dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.evidence: Core D6 behavior diverges from the acceptance criteria: `skills/ideate.md:33` still prescribes `<!-- ideation:section:{name} -->`, `commands/work.md.tmpl:140` tells the operator to use `<!-- {step}:section:{name} -->` instead of `presentation`, and `internal/cli/hook/presentation_check.go:17-24,97-103` scans `transcript_path` rather than assistant-turn content from Stop-hook stdin.

dimensions.name: test-coverage
dimensions.evidence: New branching hook logic is covered by `internal/cli/hook/presentation_check_test.go`, and the deliverable adds an integration replay in `tests/integration/test-presentation-protocol.sh`; `go test -count=1 ./...` and `sh tests/integration/test-presentation-protocol.sh` both passed in the current tree.

dimensions.name: spec-compliance
dimensions.evidence: The implementation misses explicit interface-contract details from the spec: the Stop hook input shape is transcript-based instead of assistant-content based (`internal/cli/hook/presentation_check.go:17-24`), and the required additive references in the steps’ `Supervised Transition Protocol` sections are absent entirely (`rg "Supervised Transition Protocol|Presentation" skills/{plan,spec,review}.md` shows only standalone `Presentation` notes).

dimensions.name: unplanned-changes
dimensions.evidence: `bb64f75` modifies `internal/cli/app.go` to register `presentation-check`, but `artifact-presentation-protocol` file ownership in `.furrow/rows/orchestration-delegation-contract/definition.yaml` does not include `internal/cli/app.go`, and no justification is documented.

dimensions.name: code-quality
dimensions.evidence: The new Go and shell code follows repo conventions well enough to build and test cleanly (`go test -count=1 ./...` passed; the hook uses `slog`, tests are conventional, and the shell script quotes variables and runs under `sh`).
